terms.definition: Dimensionless wall distance: y⁺ = u_τy/ν. Controls near-wall model validity. y⁺≈1: wall-resolved; y⁺=30-300: log-law (wall functions).
Reynolds-Averaged Navier-Stokes. Solves for time-mean flow; models turbulent fluctuations via eddy viscosity closure.
Large Eddy Simulation. Resolves large eddies; models subgrid scales. High accuracy but very high cost.
Menter's Shear Stress Transport model. Blends k-ω (near wall) and k-ε (freestream). Best for adverse pressure gradient and separation.
Imbalance in discretised governing equations. Dropping 3-4 orders is necessary but not sufficient for convergence.
Thin prismatic cells normal to wall. Required for y+ control, wall shear, and heat transfer accuracy.
Grid Convergence Index. Richardson extrapolation estimate of discretisation error between mesh refinement levels.
Semi-Implicit Method for Pressure-Linked Equations. Segregated p-V coupling. Default for steady incompressible flow.
Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy: V·Δt/Δx. Explicit stability condition: CFL ≤ 1.
I = u'/U [%]. Inlet BC for turbulence models. Pipe flow: 3-5%; clean freestream: 0.1-1%.
Semi-empirical formula bridging viscous sublayer to log-law. Requires y⁺=30-300. Used with k-ε models.
Models Reynolds stresses proportional to mean strain rate via eddy viscosity μ_t. Assumption of all two-equation RANS models.
